addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: This function already has a spec and I have a memory note about it. Let me check the memory.

content.content:
1	---
2	name: cbss-depth200-vacuity
3	description: "CalculateBlockSymbolSize 0/6 kills under hardcoded cbmc --depth 200, inherent vacuity not weak spec"
4	metadata: 
5	  node_type: memory
6	  type: project
7	  originSessionId: d71f2b4d-16a7-4701-85a6-94f87864f370
8	---
9	
10	`CalculateBlockSymbolSize` (Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code/zopfli.c) is a thin dispatcher: `if (lstart + ZOPFLI_NUM_LL*3 > lend)` → CalculateBlockSymbolSizeSmall, else ZopfliLZ77GetHistogram + CalculateBlockSymbolSizeGivenCounts. Verifies at --depth 200 with a union-of-callees contract (5 mandatory `is_fresh`: ll_lengths, d_lengths, lz77, lz77->litlens, lz77->dists, + token-validity forall) and ensures `>= ll_lengths[256]`.
11	
12	All 6 avocado mutants are on the branch guard. Sibling of [[cbssgc-depth200-vacuity]] / [[cbsss-depth200-vacuity]].
13	
14	**Why 0/6 under --depth 200 (don't chase):**
15	- The 5 `is_fresh` are all required to discharge the replaced callees' own `is_fresh` requires — can't drop or swap to `r_ok` (r_ok makes the CBSSS is_fresh obligation FAIL verification; tested). is_fresh prologue pushes the in-branch guard-image asserts to a depth-330 frontier (SUCCESS@320, FAILED@350).
16	- Callees CBSSS+CBSSGC both require `lz77->size <= 3` → caller must require it → `lend <= 3` → else (histogram) branch unreachable AND the boundary `lstart+3*NUM_LL == lend` unreachable. So `>=`, `!=`, and the `lstart - ...` mutant are **equivalent** (differ from `>` only at that boundary) — unkillable at ANY depth.
17	- The `<`, `<=`, `==` mutants divert all states into the else arm; the else-arm `__CPROVER_assert(lstart+3*NUM_LL <= lend)` kills them, but only at depth ≥350 (vacuous @200).
18	
19	Theoretical max 3/6 (only `<`,`<=`,`==`) and only above depth ~320; under the mandated --depth 200 it is inherently 0/6. Kill script: /app/kill_cbss.sh. See [[avocado-depth200-vacuity]].
20

file.content:
---
name: cbss-depth200-vacuity
description: "CalculateBlockSymbolSize 0/6 kills under hardcoded cbmc --depth 200, inherent vacuity not weak spec"
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: d71f2b4d-16a7-4701-85a6-94f87864f370
---

`CalculateBlockSymbolSize` (Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code/zopfli.c) is a thin dispatcher: `if (lstart + ZOPFLI_NUM_LL*3 > lend)` → CalculateBlockSymbolSizeSmall, else ZopfliLZ77GetHistogram + CalculateBlockSymbolSizeGivenCounts. Verifies at --depth 200 with a union-of-callees contract (5 mandatory `is_fresh`: ll_lengths, d_lengths, lz77, lz77->litlens, lz77->dists, + token-validity forall) and ensures `>= ll_lengths[256]`.

All 6 avocado mutants are on the branch guard. Sibling of [[cbssgc-depth200-vacuity]] / [[cbsss-depth200-vacuity]].

**Why 0/6 under --depth 200 (don't chase):**
- The 5 `is_fresh` are all required to discharge the replaced callees' own `is_fresh` requires — can't drop or swap to `r_ok` (r_ok makes the CBSSS is_fresh obligation FAIL verification; tested). is_fresh prologue pushes the in-branch guard-image asserts to a depth-330 frontier (SUCCESS@320, FAILED@350).
- Callees CBSSS+CBSSGC both require `lz77->size <= 3` → caller must require it → `lend <= 3` → else (histogram) branch unreachable AND the boundary `lstart+3*NUM_LL == lend` unreachable. So `>=`, `!=`, and the `lstart - ...` mutant are **equivalent** (differ from `>` only at that boundary) — unkillable at ANY depth.
- The `<`, `<=`, `==` mutants divert all states into the else arm; the else-arm `__CPROVER_assert(lstart+3*NUM_LL <= lend)` kills them, but only at depth ≥350 (vacuous @200).

Theoretical max 3/6 (only `<`,`<=`,`==`) and only above depth ~320; under the mandated --depth 200 it is inherently 0/6. Kill script: /app/kill_cbss.sh. See [[avocado-depth200-vacuity]].

content.text: My memory documents this as a known inherent-vacuity case (0/6 under the mandated `--depth 200`). But let me confirm the current state still holds rather than trusting memory blindly. Let me check the verification setup.

content.content:
#!/bin/bash
set -u
SRC=/app/Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code/zopfli.c
FUNCTION=CalculateBlockSymbolSize
WORK=/app/_kill_cbss
rm -rf $WORK; mkdir -p $WORK; cd $WORK

# All mutants act on the branch guard in CalculateBlockSymbolSize.  The same
# text also occurs in ZopfliLZ77GetHistogram / CalculateBlockSymbolSizeGivenCounts,
# but those bodies are replaced by contracts here, so a global swap is harmless.
ORIG=(
'if (lstart + ZOPFLI_NUM_LL * 3 > lend)'
'if (lstart + ZOPFLI_NUM_LL * 3 > lend)'
'if (lstart + ZOPFLI_NUM_LL * 3 > lend)'
'if (lstart + ZOPFLI_NUM_LL * 3 > lend)'
'if (lstart + ZOPFLI_NUM_LL * 3 > lend)'
'if (lstart + ZOPFLI_NUM_LL * 3 > lend)'
)
MUT=(
'if (lstart + ZOPFLI_NUM_LL * 3 < lend)'
'if (lstart + ZOPFLI_NUM_LL * 3 <= lend)'
'if (lstart + ZOPFLI_NUM_LL * 3 >= lend)'
'if (lstart + ZOPFLI_NUM_LL * 3 == lend)'
'if (lstart + ZOPFLI_NUM_LL * 3 != lend)'
'if (lstart - ZOPFLI_NUM_LL * 3 > lend)'
)

killed=0
for idx in "${!MUT[@]}"; do
  rm -f m.goto mc.goto mutated.c
  cp $SRC mutated.c
  O="${ORIG[$idx]}"; M="${MUT[$idx]}"; O="$O" M="$M" perl -i -pe 's/\Q$ENV{O}\E/$ENV{M}/' mutated.c
  if ! grep -qF "$M" mutated.c; then echo "APPLYFAIL $idx"; continue; fi
  if ! goto-cc -o m.goto mutated.c -I /app/Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code --function $FUNCTION 2>/tmp/cc.err; then echo "CCFAIL $idx"; cat /tmp/cc.err; continue; fi
  goto-instrument --partial-loops --unwind 5 m.goto m.goto 2>/dev/null
  goto-instrument --replace-call-with-contract CalculateBlockSymbolSizeSmall --replace-call-with-contract ZopfliLZ77GetHistogram --replace-call-with-contract CalculateBlockSymbolSizeGivenCounts --enforce-contract $FUNCTION m.goto mc.goto 2>/dev/null
  out=$(cbmc mc.goto --function $FUNCTION --depth 200 2>&1 | grep -E "VERIFICATION (SUCCESSFUL|FAILED)")
  if echo "$out" | grep -q SUCCESSFUL; then
    echo "SURVIVED  $idx: $M"
  else
    echo "KILLED    $idx: $M  [$out]"
    killed=$((killed+1))
  fi
done
echo "=== Killed $killed / ${#MUT[@]} ==="
---SCRIPTS---
/app/kill_addlz77block.sh
/app/kill_cbss.sh
/app/kill_cts.sh
/app/kill_gft.sh
/app/kill_tohfr.sh
/app/kill_zlz77gbr.sh
---harness---
contracts-assigns.md
contracts-frees.md
contracts-function-pointer-predicates.md
contracts-history-variables.md
contracts-memory-predicates.md
contracts-quantifiers.md
contracts-requires-ensures.md
running-cbmc-directly.md

input.command:
cat > /app/_verify_cbss.sh <<'EOF'
#!/bin/bash
set -u
SRC=/app/Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code/zopfli.c
FUNCTION=CalculateBlockSymbolSize
WORK=/app/_verify_cbss
rm -rf $WORK; mkdir -p $WORK; cd $WORK
cp $SRC mutated.c
goto-cc -o m.goto mutated.c -I /app/Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code --function $FUNCTION 2>/tmp/cc.err || { echo CCFAIL; cat /tmp/cc.err; exit 1; }
goto-instrument --partial-loops --unwind 5 m.goto m.goto 2>/dev/null
goto-instrument --replace-call-with-contract CalculateBlockSymbolSizeSmall --replace-call-with-contract ZopfliLZ77GetHistogram --replace-call-with-contract CalculateBlockSymbolSizeGivenCounts --enforce-contract $FUNCTION m.goto mc.goto 2>/dev/null
cbmc mc.goto --function $FUNCTION --depth 200 2>&1 | grep -E "VERIFICATION (SUCCESSFUL|FAILED)"
EOF
chmod +x /app/_verify_cbss.sh && /app/_verify_cbss.sh

content.content:
327	static size_t CalculateBlockSymbolSizeSmall(const unsigned *ll_lengths,
328	                                            const unsigned *d_lengths,
329	                                            const ZopfliLZ77Store *lz77,
330	                                            size_t lstart, size_t lend)
331	// clang-format off
332	/* ll_lengths is a literal/length code-length table (ZOPFLI_NUM_LL entries) and
333	   d_lengths a distance code-length table (ZOPFLI_NUM_D entries).  The body reads
334	   ll_lengths at litlens[i] (< 259), at the length symbol (257..285) and at the
335	   end symbol 256, and d_lengths at the distance symbol (0..29); all of these
336	   indices stay in bounds for the declared table sizes. */
337	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(ll_lengths, ZOPFLI_NUM_LL * sizeof(*ll_lengths)))
338	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(d_lengths, ZOPFLI_NUM_D * sizeof(*d_lengths)))
339	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(lz77, sizeof(*lz77)))
340	/* Keep the symbol window small so the bounded pipeline reaches the loop body and
341	   the lstart==lend single-iteration paths the loop-bound mutants depend on. */
342	__CPROVER_requires(lz77->size <= 3)
343	__CPROVER_requires(lstart <= lend && lend <= lz77->size)
344	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(lz77->litlens, lz77->size * sizeof(*lz77->litlens)))
345	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(lz77->dists, lz77->size * sizeof(*lz77->dists)))
346	/* Within the processed window each entry is a valid DEFLATE token: the lit/len
347	   value indexes the 259-entry length tables, and a non-literal (dist != 0)
348	   carries a real length (>= 3) and an in-range distance (<= 32768). */
349	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_forall {
350	    size_t k; (lstart <= k && k < lend) ==>
351	        (lz77->litlens[k] <= 258 &&
352	         (lz77->dists[k] == 0 ||
353	          (lz77->litlens[k] >= 3 && lz77->dists[k] <= 32768))) })
354	__CPROVER_assigns()
355	/* The end symbol's code length is always included in the total. */
356	__CPROVER_ensures(__CPROVER_return_value >= ll_lengths[256])
357	// clang-format on
358	{
359	    size_t result = 0;
360	    size_t i;
361	    for (i = lstart; i < lend; i++)
362	    {
363	        /* Iteration-local image of the loop bound: reached inside the body (unlike
364	           the post-state ensures), so it is the killer the bounded --depth pipeline
365	           can actually reach for the over-running loop-bound mutants. */
366	        __CPROVER_assert(i >= lstart && i < lend, "CBSSS: index stays in [lstart,lend)");
367	        assert(i < lz77->size);
368	        assert(lz77->litlens[i] < 259);
369	        if (lz77->dists[i] == 0)
370	        {
371	            result += ll_lengths[lz77->litlens[i]];
372	        }
373	        else
374	        {
375	            int ll_symbol = ZopfliGetLengthSymbol(lz77->litlens[i]);
376	            int d_symbol = ZopfliGetDistSymbol(lz77->dists[i]);
377	            result += ll_lengths[ll_symbol];
378	            result += d_lengths[d_symbol];
379	            result += ZopfliGetLengthSymbolExtraBits(ll_symbol);
380	            result += ZopfliGetDistSymbolExtraBits(d_symbol);
381	        }
382	    }
383	    result += ll_lengths[256]; /*end symbol*/
384	    return result;
385	}
386	
387	/*
388	Same as CalculateBlockSymbolSize, but with the histogram provided by the caller.
389	*/
390	static size_t CalculateBlockSymbolSizeGivenCounts(const size_t *ll_counts,
391	                                                  const size_t *d_counts,
392	                                                  const unsigned *ll_lengths,
393	                                                  const unsigned *d_lengths,
394	                                                  const ZopfliLZ77Store *lz77,
395	                                                  size_t lstart, size_t lend)
396	// clang-format off
397	/* The histogram path (else branch) reads ll_counts at 0..255 and 257..285 and
398	   ll_lengths at 0..285 plus the end symbol 256 (all < ZOPFLI_NUM_LL), and
399	   d_counts / d_lengths at 0..29 (< ZOPFLI_NUM_D); these tables must be full. */
400	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(ll_counts, ZOPFLI_NUM_LL * sizeof(*ll_counts)))
401	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(d_counts, ZOPFLI_NUM_D * sizeof(*d_counts)))

content.text: The memory's analysis is thorough, but it only documents trying the `r_ok` swap. One untried idea: tightening `lz77->size <= 3` to a smaller bound would shrink the `is_fresh(litlens/dists)` allocations and the `forall` range, potentially pulling the else-arm assert under depth 200 to kill the 3 divert-to-else mutants (`<`, `<=`, `==`). Let me experiment on a scratch copy first.

# In CalculateBlockSymbolSize only, change size<=3 to size<=1. That text appears in multiple funcs; target line 1889.
perl -i -pe 's/__CPROVER_requires\(lz77->size <= 3\)/__CPROVER_requires(lz77->size <= 1)/ if $. == 1889' z.c
grep -n "lz77->size <= 1" z.c

input.command:
cat > /tmp/exp/run.sh <<'EOF'
#!/bin/bash
set -u
SRC=/tmp/exp/z.c
FUNCTION=CalculateBlockSymbolSize
cd /tmp/exp
build () {
  cp "$1" mutated.c
  goto-cc -o m.goto mutated.c -I /app/Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code --function $FUNCTION 2>/tmp/cc.err || { echo CCFAIL; cat /tmp/cc.err; return 1; }
  goto-instrument --partial-loops --unwind 5 m.goto m.goto 2>/dev/null
  goto-instrument --replace-call-with-contract CalculateBlockSymbolSizeSmall --replace-call-with-contract ZopfliLZ77GetHistogram --replace-call-with-contract CalculateBlockSymbolSizeGivenCounts --enforce-contract $FUNCTION m.goto mc.goto 2>/dev/null
}
echo "=== unmutated verify ==="
build $SRC && cbmc mc.goto --function $FUNCTION --depth 200 2>&1 | grep -E "VERIFICATION (SUCCESSFUL|FAILED)"
echo "=== mutants ==="
MUT=('if (lstart + ZOPFLI_NUM_LL * 3 < lend)' 'if (lstart + ZOPFLI_NUM_LL * 3 <= lend)' 'if (lstart + ZOPFLI_NUM_LL * 3 == lend)')
killed=0
for M in "${MUT[@]}"; do
  cp $SRC mut.c
  M="$M" perl -i -pe 's/\Qif (lstart + ZOPFLI_NUM_LL * 3 > lend)\E/$ENV{M}/' mut.c
  build mut.c || continue
  out=$(cbmc mc.goto --function $FUNCTION --depth 200 2>&1 | grep -E "VERIFICATION (SUCCESSFUL|FAILED)")
  if echo "$out" | grep -q SUCCESSFUL; then echo "SURVIVED: $M"; else echo "KILLED: $M"; killed=$((killed+1)); fi
done
echo "killed div-mutants: $killed/3"
EOF
chmod +x /tmp/exp/run.sh && /tmp/exp/run.sh

---
name: cbss-depth200-vacuity
description: "CalculateBlockSymbolSize 0/6 kills under hardcoded cbmc --depth 200, inherent vacuity not weak spec"
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: d71f2b4d-16a7-4701-85a6-94f87864f370
---

`CalculateBlockSymbolSize` (Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code/zopfli.c) is a thin dispatcher: `if (lstart + ZOPFLI_NUM_LL*3 > lend)` → CalculateBlockSymbolSizeSmall, else ZopfliLZ77GetHistogram + CalculateBlockSymbolSizeGivenCounts. Verifies at --depth 200 with a union-of-callees contract (5 mandatory `is_fresh`: ll_lengths, d_lengths, lz77, lz77->litlens, lz77->dists, + token-validity forall) and ensures `>= ll_lengths[256]`.

All 6 avocado mutants are on the branch guard. Sibling of [[cbssgc-depth200-vacuity]] / [[cbsss-depth200-vacuity]].

**Why 0/6 under --depth 200 (don't chase):**
- The 5 `is_fresh` are all required to discharge the replaced callees' own `is_fresh` requires — can't drop or swap to `r_ok` (r_ok makes the CBSSS is_fresh obligation FAIL verification; tested). is_fresh prologue pushes the in-branch guard-image asserts to a depth-330 frontier (SUCCESS@320, FAILED@350).
- Callees CBSSS+CBSSGC both require `lz77->size <= 3` → caller must require it → `lend <= 3` → else (histogram) branch unreachable AND the boundary `lstart+3*NUM_LL == lend` unreachable. So `>=`, `!=`, and the `lstart - ...` mutant are **equivalent** (differ from `>` only at that boundary) — unkillable at ANY depth.
- The `<`, `<=`, `==` mutants divert all states into the else arm; the else-arm `__CPROVER_assert(lstart+3*NUM_LL <= lend)` kills them, but only at depth ≥350 (vacuous @200).

Theoretical max 3/6 (only `<`,`<=`,`==`) and only above depth ~320; under the mandated --depth 200 it is inherently 0/6. Kill script: /app/kill_cbss.sh. See [[avocado-depth200-vacuity]].

content.text:
## Summary

`CalculateBlockSymbolSize` in `/app/Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code/zopfli.c` **already verifies** (VERIFICATION SUCCESSFUL at the mandated `--depth 200`, with its callees replaced by contracts), and its specification is already as strong as the depth budget allows.

**Kill score: 0/6 — this is inherent, not a weak spec.** All 6 avocado mutants act on the dispatcher's branch guard `if (lstart + ZOPFLI_NUM_LL * 3 > lend)`:
- **3 are equivalent mutants** (`>=`, `!=`, `lstart - ...`): under the `lz77->size <= 3` regime, `lstart + 3*288 > lend` is always true and the boundary is unreachable, so these are semantically identical to `>` and unkillable at any depth.
- **3 divert-to-else mutants** (`<`, `<=`, `==`) are caught by the else-arm assertion, but only at depth ≥350 — past the mandated `--depth 200`.

I made one genuine new attempt beyond my prior analysis: tightening `lz77->size <= 3` → `<= 1` on a scratch copy to shrink the precondition prologue and pull the killing assertion under depth 200. It didn't help (still 0/3) — the depth bottleneck is the **fixed-size** `is_fresh` on `ll_lengths` (288 entries) and `d_lengths` (32 entries), which the callee contracts mandate and which window-size tuning can't reduce.

I left the source unchanged (line 1889 still `<= 3`), did all experiments in `/tmp`, and recorded the failed size-tightening attempt in memory so it won't be retried.

lastPrompt: Verify CalculateBlockSymbolSize in /app/Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code/zopfli.c; if it already verifies, strengthen its specification by increasing the kill score by killing mutants.
